displayName: Saba University School of Medicine — Caribbean
shortDescription: MD program · NVAO accredited · Basic science on Saba · Clinical rotations in US & Canada

policyAlert.summary: Verify current tuition, intake dates, and admission requirements on saba.edu before quoting clients. Indian students: confirm NEET eligibility and NMC guidance separately.

alert.title: MD vs MBBS naming
alert.body: SUSOM awards a Doctor of Medicine (MD) — counsel Indian clients on FMGE/NExT pathway; do not describe as Indian-style MBBS without explaining licensing steps.

about.label: Description
about.value: Saba University School of Medicine (SUSOM), established 1992, offers a 4-year Doctor of Medicine program. Five semesters of basic sciences on the island of Saba (Caribbean Netherlands), followed by five semesters of clinical rotations at affiliated teaching hospitals in the United States, with select elective sites in Canada.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Bachelor's degree or equivalent pre-med foundation · English proficiency · Meet SUSOM admission requirements · Indian applicants: verify NEET eligibility with current NMC rules

about.label: Accreditation highlight
about.value: NVAO accreditation — standards deemed comparable to US medical schools by US DOE NCFMEA. Only Caribbean medical school with European NVAO accreditation per SUSOM.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: Saba University School of Medicine (admissions) · Netherlands / Dutch Caribbean immigration for study · US immigration for clinical years

about.label: After graduation
about.value: USMLE Steps 1 & 2 CK · NRMP residency match · FMGE/NExT for India practice · Canadian residency pathways for eligible graduates

accreditation: Accreditation Organisation of the Netherlands and Flanders (NVAO)
Recognised by World Federation of Medical Education (WFME) via NVAO
US Department of Education NCFMEA — standards comparable to US medical schools
State approvals for clinical rotations in the US — verify current list on saba.edu
mbbs.programDuration: 4 years (10 semesters): 5 basic science on Saba + 5 clinical in US/Canada
mbbs.clinicalTrainingNotes: Clinical medicine at affiliated US teaching hospitals; select elective rotation sites in Canada. Verify current affiliate list on official site.
mbbs.campusNotes: Compact island campus in The Bottom, Saba. Housing and student life resources published on saba.edu. Basic science years are island-based; clinical years relocate to rotation cities.

relatedPrograms.name: Doctor of Medicine (MD) Program
relatedPrograms.summary: Primary program: basic sciences on Saba followed by clinical rotations in the US. USMLE preparation integrated; 98% first-time Step 1 pass rate (2024 per SUSOM).

relatedPrograms.name: Gateway Medical Program
relatedPrograms.summary: Conditional acceptance preparatory course; successful completion leads to next MD entering class. Also offered via Medical University of the Americas (MUA).
relatedPrograms.notes: For students needing academic preparation before MD entry.

relatedPrograms.name: Pre-Medical Master's Program
relatedPrograms.summary: Accelerated pre-med for career changers; MSc awarded by Medical University of the Americas (MUA), then apply to SUSOM MD program.

familyOptions.spouseCanAccompany: Possible during basic science on Saba but subject to Dutch Caribbean / Netherlands entry rules for dependants — verify current immigration policy.
familyOptions.spouseWorkRights: Spouse work authorization on Saba is very limited; small island economy. Do not promise employment. During US clinical years, spouse may need separate US visa strategy.
familyOptions.childrenCanAccompany: Case-by-case — verify dependant visa rules and schooling availability on Saba (small island).
familyOptions.childrenNotes: Schooling options limited on Saba; plan carefully for family relocation.
familyOptions.additionalFundsRequired: Additional living funds and health insurance for each dependant — verify with admissions and immigration counsel.
familyOptions.visaRoute: Student visa/residence for principal applicant; dependants require separate permits per Dutch Caribbean rules.
restrictions: Do not promise spouse work on Saba
Clinical years in US — family visa strategy differs from island basic science period
Verify all family immigration with official sources before client commitment

practicePathways.summary: SUSOM MD graduates pursue residency in the US and Canada; Indian nationals must clear FMGE/NExT for licensure in India. SUSOM publishes USMLE pass rates and residency placement statistics.

india.fmgeNext: Indian citizens with foreign MD must qualify FMGE (transitioning to NExT) and meet NMC requirements before provisional/ permanent registration in India.
details: Verify institution status on NMC foreign medical institutions list before enrollment.
Degree is MD (US-style), not 5.5-year Indian MBBS — set client expectations early.
Internship/clinical hours must meet NMC criteria — verify with current NMC notifications.
Never guarantee FMGE/NExT pass or India registration.

usCanada.summary: Graduates eligible for USMLE Step 1 & Step 2 CK; 98% first-time pass rate reported for 2024. 99% three-year residency placement rate (2023–26 cohorts per SUSOM).
details: Basic science on Saba → US clinical rotations → USMLE → NRMP Match.
Canadian students may pay tuition in CAD; some elective rotations in Canada.
Residency placement in US and Canada — verify latest match statistics on saba.edu.

recognition.who: NVAO is recognised by WFME; SUSOM states European accreditation comparable to US standards via NCFMEA.
recognition.nmc: Counselors must verify current NMC eligible colleges list — status can change; check before each intake.

restrictions: Never guarantee US residency match or India medical license
Verify NMC list at time of admission

documentChecklistSections.label: Admission documents
items: NEET scorecard (Indian applicants — if required under current NMC rules)

documentChecklistSections.label: Visa & immigration (basic science — Saba)
items: Proof of tuition payment or financial guarantee
Visa application forms per Dutch Caribbean / Netherlands consulate

documentChecklistSections.label: US clinical years (additional)
items: US visa strategy (typically F-1 or as advised by university for rotations)
SEVIS / I-20 if applicable for US portion
Health insurance for US clinical sites
Updated financial proof for US living costs

applicant.summary: During basic science on Saba, students must maintain valid student immigration status. Paid employment is extremely limited on the small island; focus is full-time study. US clinical years governed by US student visa rules.
details: Basic science (Saba): do not assume off-campus work — verify student residence permit conditions.
Clinical rotations (US): F-1 or university-advised status; any employment must comply with US immigration rules.
University may offer limited on-campus roles — verify with SUSOM student services.
restrictions: Unauthorized work violates visa status in Netherlands/US portions
Never advise clients to work illegally to fund tuition

spouse.summary: Spouse/partner work rights are very limited on Saba. US clinical phase requires separate US immigration planning for dependants.
details: Island economy cannot support typical spouse employment expectations.
Plan finances assuming single-earner household during basic science.
restrictions: Do not promise spouse employment on Saba or during rotations without visa approval

fullCostBreakdown.title: Full cost breakdown — Saba University School of Medicine
fullCostBreakdown.disclaimer: Indicative structure for counselor discussions. Tuition changes each academic year — always quote from https://www.saba.edu/admissions/tuition-and-fees/ before client commitment.

items.label: Total program tuition (indicative)
items.notes: Sep 2026 matriculants — verify on saba.edu before quoting

totals.label: Indicative total program budget
totals.notes: Use www.saba.edu tuition page; add 15% buffer for currency and fee increases.

eligibility.criterion: Bachelor's degree or qualifying pre-med coursework
NEET qualified (Indian applicants — verify current NMC rule)
eligibility.note: Check NMC notification at admission year

redFlagsBanner: If client expects guaranteed US residency or India license without FMGE/NExT, reset expectations before taking fees.

redFlags.title: Guaranteed residency promise
redFlags.description: Agents promising US residency or 100% match.
redFlags.fix: Quote SUSOM published stats only; no guarantees

redFlags.title: NMC list not verified
redFlags.description: Enrollment without checking current NMC foreign medical institutions list.

redFlags.title: Underestimating clinical-year costs
redFlags.description: Budgeting only for Saba island living.
redFlags.fix: Include US rotation city living costs

redFlags.title: Family relocation without visa plan
redFlags.fix: Immigration counsel before promising family join

faqs.a: NVAO accredited; WFME recognised; US NCFMEA comparable standards — see saba.edu/why-saba/
4 years (10 semesters): 5 basic science on Saba + 5 clinical in US.
January, May, and September per saba.edu.

faqs.q: Can Indian students apply after NEET?
faqs.a: Verify current NMC rules for foreign medical study — NEET qualification may be required.
Doctor of Medicine (MD) — US-style medical degree.
Yes for Indian licensure after foreign MD — verify current NMC process.
SUSOM reports 98% first-time Step 1 and Step 2 CK pass (2024).
SUSOM reports 99% three-year residency placement rate — verify latest on graduate success page.
Island of Saba, Dutch Caribbean.
Affiliated US hospitals; select Canada electives.
Yes — entire MD program in English.
Very limited — do not promise spouse employment.

Check saba.edu/admissions/scholarships/ for current offerings.

See saba.edu/student-experience/housing/

No — never guarantee admission, visa, residency, or licensure.
See Checklist tab — admission, visa, clinical, family sections.
Plan F-1 or university-guided status for US rotations — case specific.
Verify institution on NMC list at time of enrollment.
Check World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS).
Not relied upon — maintain full-time student status.
